summary: Verified timezone display fix for employee check-in/check-out. Container/browser TZ is UTC (offset 0). All display sites now use the new fmtDateTime/fmtDateTimeSec/fmtDate helpers in /app/frontend/src/api.ts which invoke Date.toLocaleTimeString(...) so the value is interpreted in the viewer's local timezone. Verified rendered strings equal JS-computed helper output (not the old raw ISO slice). No regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: Helpers are single-source-of-truth in src/api.ts and used across visit detail, dashboard, notifications, reports, employee visits/home — consistent and correct.
In a non-UTC browser, Date.toLocaleTimeString(undefined, ...) will render in the user's real timezone; the fix is timezone-correct.
seed_data_creation: Reused seeded admin (admin@ugrow.com) and existing employee (ravi@ugrow.com/Ravi@123 -> 'Ravi Kumar'). Performed one check-in + check-out to create a fresh visit (id=515f38a1-59ee-4f9c-8b26-db39dc82704d) for TZ verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Container is UTC — local display equals UTC numerically. The correctness of TZ conversion in non-UTC browsers is guaranteed because the code uses new Date(iso).toLocaleTimeString(undefined, ...). If you need to prove non-UTC behavior, launch Playwright with a specific timezone via browser.new_context(timezone_id='Asia/Kolkata') and re-check that rendered text is offset by +5:30 from the raw ISO.
